s.f. = tūlī, q.v. s.f. A fibrous stick used as a brush by weavers to clean the threads of the woof (or used as a brush generally, cf. tur); a painter's brush or pencil; a rod, &c., dipped into a crucible to ascertain whether the contents are in fusion.

adj. & s.m. The fourth, a fourth part; whose power extends on all four sides, mighty; — Brahmā or the universal spirit; the fourth state of the soul (according to the Vedānta philosophy), the state in which the soul has become one with the universal spirit.
s.m. 'A Turk'; a Turkish, or a Mohammadan soldier; a Mohammadan; a barbarian; a plunderer; a vagabond; (fig.) a beautiful-faced person

s.m. turk-tāzī, s.f. A rapid raid or plundering expedition; inroad, depredation, attack ( = tāz); — feigned anger (of a lover); walking affectedly or with a swinging gait
s.m. A captivating or killing eye
adj. Of a savage disposition, ruthless, cruel; sly; wicked, depraved.

def: s.f. (fig.) Beautiful women.

adj. = turkāna, q.v. adj. Like a Turk, Turk-like; Turkish.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a Turk; Turkish, Turk-like; — a horse of Turkish breed; — s.f. The Turkish language; Turk-like qualities or habits, oppression, haughtiness, insolence, &c. (see turk)
subs: adv. Insolence for insolence, &c.; tit for tat; a Rowland for an Oliver (e.g. jawāb turkī ba-turkī denā, To answer insolence with insolence, to give tit for tat)
(-kī), One's pride or haughtiness to come to an end; to be humbled or abased; to be undone or ruined; to be all over (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترکی (turkī). By surface analysis, ترک (turk) + ـی (-ī).

ety: S. त्रुटित+कः, or perf. part. of ṭūṭ-nā

def: s.f. A parrot, paroquet; — the small singing bird Loxia rosea, or Carpodacus erythrinus; — (met.) a sweet-tongued or eloquent speaker
subs: (apnī), 'To cause (one's) t̤ūt̤ī to sing'; to acquire fame, get a name
(-kisī kī), 'The t̤ūt̤ī (of anyone) to be singing'; to be in good repute; to gain the ear (of one in authority); to be winning golden opinions; to acquire influence or authority
(-kisī-ke), To rub or twist the parrot-like lips (of one, — a phrase used by way of an affectionate or friendly admonition).

def: s.m. Blue vitriol, sulphate of copper, tutty.
s.m. = ṭoṭkā, q.v. s.m. A charm, spell, an amulet, a philter, a superstitious remedy (cf. ṭāṭak; syn. afsūn).
part. adj. Lisping, stammering, &c. ( = totlā).
s.f. Lisping; stammering, stuttering.
v.n. To speak indistinctly (as a child), to lisp; to sputter (in speaking); to stammer, stutter.

ety: ṭūṭ˚ = Prk. तुट्ट(इ), or टुट्ट(इ); S. त्रुट्य(ति), rt. त्रुट्
def: v.n. To be broken, fractured, cracked, damaged, &c.; to be severed, sundered, dissolved (as a partnership); to break, snap in two, burst, fail (as a bank, or as a supply), to fall short, be deficient or scarce; to fall into arrears, be unrealized; to break out, fall out, befall, happen; to break loose, break forth, break in (upon, -por), rush in, make a rush (upon), fall (upon), attack, charge, assault (in these senses the more usual form is ṭūṭ-paṛnā); to fall in torrents (as rain), be poured forth, be rained or showered copiously; to be lavish (of money, &c.), to spend lavishly or freely; to be lost, spent; to be ruined, be reduced to proverty; to feel pains in the bones or joints; to pine; to fall ill; to become weak or infirm, to break up (in these senses ṭūṭ-jānā is the more common form)
subs: (-par), To be collected in crowds; to break or rush in (upon), &c., see ṭūṭnā
v.n. (intens.) See ṭūṭnā, at the beginning and towards the end
v.n. To be weary, to pine away; to suffer pains in the bones; to be separated (from, -se); to be distressed, be reduced to poverty
v.n. To fall in torrents (as rain, &c.), to be poured out or forth lavishly or copiously (as bounties, &c.).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀝𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀝𑀇 (ṭuṭṭaï, “to break”), ultimately from Sanskrit तृद् (tṛd, “to cleave, split”). Cognate to Punjabi ਟੁੱਟਣਾ (ṭuṭṭaṇā), Gujarati તૂટવું (tūṭvũ), Marathi तुटणे (tuṭṇe), Bengali টুটা (ṭuṭa), Odia ତୁଟିବା (tuṭibā).

def: v.n. prop. ṭūṭnā, q.v.
v.n. = tutlānā, q.v. v.n. To speak indistinctly (as a child), to lisp; to sputter (in speaking); to stammer, stutter.
s.m. The cooing of a dove; — adv. Alone, all forlorn.
s.m. The cooing of a dove; — adv. Alone, all forlorn.
s.m. Blue vitriol, sulphate of copper (esp. as an ointment or medical application to the eyes); a collyrium extracted from the Amomum zanthorrhiza
subs: (˚tha+an˚), s.m. Blue vitriol as an aujan or medical application to the eyes.

s.f. Skin; bark, &c. ( = tvać, q.v.)

s.m. Leaving, quitting, abandoning, forsaking, deserting, parting (from); relinquishing, renouncing; abandonment, desertion; relinquishment, renunciation, resignation, abdication; separation (from), divorce; self-devotion

ex:
چاند پر جو کمند ڈالتے ہیں
مجھ سے کہتے ہیں زندگی بھی تیاگ
cānd par jo kamand ḍālte ha͠i
mujh se kahte ha͠i zindgī bhī tyāg

part. adj. Left, abandoned, forsaken, deserted; resigned, relinquished, renounced; rejected, discarded, thrown away (as useless or as reprobate); — one who is abandoned, &c.

ety: Prk. तेम्वइ or तेवइ loc. sing. of तेम्व, and तेम, तेँव, तिम, तिँव; S. तावत्
def: adv. (correl. to jyoṅ), Thus, so, then, in like manner, like, as; then, at the same time, forthwith

= اُ u, in Urdū. (A common method of writing among those who write Urdū; e.g. اوترنا = اُترنا utarnā
subs: therefore, beginning with او not to be found below, see s.v. اُ u.).

def: (dim. of ʻabd), A servant, slave; a worshipper, devotee.

v.t. To vomit, reject.

subs: v.n. To commence boiling, get into a state of ebullition
v.t. To boil, cause to boil ( = ubālnā).

def: (perf. part. of ubālnā), part. adj. Lit. 'boiled'; boiled in water simply, unseasoned; bleached, whitened.

ety: ubal˚ = Prk. उव्वल(इ) = S. उज्ज्वल(ति), rt. उद्+ज्वल्
def: v.n. To go upwards, to rise, ascend, mount (to); to swell, flow over; to boil, boil over, bubble up; to swell with pride or rage, to be puffed up, be inflamed; to abound; to be flushed (with); to overflow, be flooded, be inundated. — ubal ćalnā, v.n. To flow or run over, boil over; to begin to swell or rise, &c.
v.t. To boil, to make boil.
wety: Urdu verb set ابلنا ابالنا ابلانا ابلوانا Inherited from Sanskrit *उद्वालयति (*udvālayati) (compare वालयति (vālayati)), Proto-Indo-European *wel-. Compare Punjabi ਉਬਾਲਣਾ (ubālṇā), Marathi उबाळा (ubāḷā).

def: v.t. To sow, plant; to cause to germinate, cause to grow; to fructify
s.m. Release, deliverance.
adj. Rough, rugged, uneven, impassable, impracticable (a road); steep, inaccessible, &c. ( = aughaṭ, q.v.)

ety: ubār˚ = Prk. उव्वारे(इ) = S. उद्वारय(ति), caus. of rt. उद्+वृ
def: v.a. To put by as a surplus or balance; to keep in reserve; — to release, deliver, liberate, set at liberty.

v.n. To be left over as a remainder or surplus, to remain over, be over; to exceed; to be kept in reserve, be put by. — ubrā-subrā, s.m. Remainder; surplus; remains, fragments, remnants; leavings; scraps; refuse.

v.n. To give forth a smell, become fetid; to turn; to smell offensive (meat or food); to rot, putrefy, become rancid or rank; to become mouldy, musty, or mildewed; to ferment; to become sticky or viscous; to become stale, or useless; to be weary, to be out of patience.
s.m. = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v. s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).

def: v.t. To cause the body to be rubbed with ubṭan, to have ubṭan rubbed on the body.
s.m. pl. (of ʻābid, q.v.), Holy men, devotees.
s.m. Heat, sultriness, languor occasioned by heat ( = ummas); condolence, sympathy.
s.m. A bear.

ety: S. उद्+भायि, caus. of rt. भी
def: v.t. To alarm, disturb.
s.m. Rising, swelling, tumefaction; plumpness; prominence; — developing; budding; acquiring fullness; rising or development of the breasts (in girls)
subs: (used in compounds), see ubhār lānā, and ubhār le-jānā, s.v. ابهارنا ubhārnā.

def: part. (of the next), Taken up, carried away, &c. — ubhārā denā, v.t. To carry off, bear away; to incite, persuade, encourage, entice
subs: v.t. To unload, &c. (see ubhārnā and ubhrānā).

ety: ubhār˚ = Prk. उब्भारे(इ) or उब्भार(इ) = S. उद्भारय(ति), caus. of rt. उद्+भृ
def: v.t. To raise up, plump up; to take up, lift; to cause to be unladen, to unlade (a cart or boat); to discharge (cargo), unship; to remove, bear away, carry off, run away with; to steal, kidnap (in these senses the more common form is ubhār-le-jānā); to rouse, stir up, incite, excite, instigate, induce, persuade; to raise up, place beyond the reach of danger or harm; to save, rescue, deliver from trouble of any kind), release, liberate, set free. — ubhār-lānā, v.t. To unload and bring; — to incite, induce, work upon, persuade, win over, seduce, draw over; to procure, obtain. — ubhār-le-jānā, v.t. To carry off, bear away, &c. (see above).

v.t. = ابهارنا ubhārnā, q.v. v.t. To raise up, plump up; to take up, lift; to cause to be unladen, to unlade (a cart or boat); to discharge (cargo), unship; to remove, bear away, carry off, run away with; to steal, kidnap (in these senses the more common form is ubhār-le-jānā); to rouse, stir up, incite, excite, instigate, induce, persuade; to raise up, place beyond the reach of danger or harm; to save, rescue, deliver from trouble of any kind), release, liberate, set free. — ubhār-lānā, v.t. To unload and bring; — to incite, induce, work upon, persuade, win over, seduce, draw over; to procure, obtain. — ubhār-le-jānā, v.t. To carry off, bear away, &c. (see above).

ety: S. उद्+भृ; see the trans. form ابهارنا ubhārnā
def: v.n. To rise, swell, be inflated; to come out fully, be developed; to rise up, form (as a pustule); to spring up, shoot, grow, increase, flourish; to run over, overflow; to be or become excited or inflamed, &c.; to feel the sexual passion; to be puffed up, be conceited; to rise from a sick bed; to recover from trouble or misfortune; to rally; to take oneself off; to take leave, depart; to make one's escape, to steal away; to come to light, be discovered or exposed, become public; to be unloaded, unladen, emptied (as a cart or boat). — ubhar ānā = ubharnā in most of its significations. — ubhar ćalnā, To be on the rise or increase; to run over, overflow; to become puffed up, vain, or conceited; to be gaining strength (after sickness, &c.), be convalescent; to be improving; to progress favourably.
= उभय ubhay, q.v.
adj. Both, the two: (in Hindī generally occurring in comp., e.g.) ubhaya-tas, adv. From both sides, on both sides, to both sides; in both cases
subs: adv. In both ways; in both cases
adv. That regards or respects both sides; common to both sides.
ex: Come here right now/at once/this instant.

def: v.n. To rise up, swell; to upheave; to feel a choking sensation (in the throat); to feel weary, tired, or disgusted.
v.n. To be oppressed with heat, to be faint or languid; to be angry or dissatisfied.
part. adj. Unladen (as a cart or boat); excited, &c.
v.t. To cause to overflow, to fill to overflowing, &c. (cf. ubhārnā, of which it is another form).
s.f. Obstacle, impediment, hindrance.
s.m. The slip-knot or noose placed round the neck of a vessel for drawing water with it.
s.f. The act of vomiting, &c. (see ubaknā).
v.t. To cause (one) to vomit; to produce vomiting, &c. (see ubaknā).
v.t. = ابالنا ubālnā, q.v. v.t. To boil, to make boil.
v.n. Lit. 'To be sown or planted'; to germinate; to grow; to be produced; to spring (as plants), sprout, shoot (cf. ugnā).
s.m. See ubrā-subrā, s.v. ubarnā. — ubrā-karnā, v.t. = ubrānā, q.v. v.a. To put by as a surplus or balance; to keep in reserve; — to release, deliver, liberate, set at liberty.
(causal of ابرنا ubarnā), = ابارنا ubārnā, q.v. v.a. To put by as a surplus or balance; to keep in reserve; — to release, deliver, liberate, set at liberty.
v.t. To cause to rot; to cause to ferment; to cause to become sticky.

ety: S. उद्+वर्तनं, 'rubbing and cleaning the body'
def: s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀯𑁆𑀯𑀝𑁆𑀝𑀡 (uvvaṭṭaṇa), from Sanskrit उद्वर्तन (udvartana).

def: s.m. = ابٿن ubṭan
subs: v.t. To anoint and rub the body with ubṭan.

def: s.f. = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v. s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).

ety: for A. عبودية, v.n. fr. عبد 'to serve'
def: s.f. Service (of God), adoration, devotion ( = ʻibādat); reverence; humble submission, submission, subjection; servitude, &c. ( = ʻabdīyat, q.v.).
s.m. Crossing, passing (a river), passing over, traversing; transportation; — going through (a book, &c.), extensive reading or study, proficiency, mastery (of a subject); — a ford, a ferry; a pass
v.t. To cause to be sown, &c.; to sow (seed).
corr. of اچهاه uććhāh, q.v. s.m. Festival, jubilee, public demonstration of joy; joy, gladness, merry-making
subs: s.m. One who exults; boaster, braggart.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), One who suddenly snatches up a thing and makes off with it; a shop-lifter, pick-pocket, pilferer, thief, knave, swindler, sharper.
s.f. = H اچکاپن उचक्कापन ućakkāpan, s.m. = H اچکاپنا उचक्कापना ućakkāpanā, s.m. Pilfering, thieving; cozening, swindling, knavery, fraud.
s.m. = H اچکاپنا उचक्कापना ućakkāpanā, s.m. = H اچکاگري उचक्कागरी ućakkāgarī, s.f. Pilfering, thieving; cozening, swindling, knavery, fraud.
s.m. = H اچکاپن उचक्कापन ućakkāpan, s.m. = H اچکاگري उचक्कागरी ućakkāgarī, s.f. Pilfering, thieving; cozening, swindling, knavery, fraud.

ety: see ućakkā; and cf. S. वञ्चक
def: v.n. To leap up, bound up, spring up, jump up; to stand on tip-toe; to slip out (of), to drop; to escape, slip away, make off; — v.t. To snatch, snap, pounce upon
subs: ućak le-jānā, v.t. To snatch away; carry off suddenly, or by stealth, or by artifice or fraud; to anticipate (as in utterance or speech).

def: v.n. To be raised or lifted up; to rise; to be jerked up.
v.n. To be separated; to separate (as skin from the flesh, or as scales, scabs, &c.).
v.t. To raise, elevate, exalt; to hoist, to lift up (a burden).
s.f. Taking of goods upon credit; a running account (as with a baniyā); credit; the goods taken on credit; — knavery, knavish tricks, fraud.
s.m. One who gives, or who takes, goods on credit; creditor; debtor; customer; — ućāptī-lekhā, s.m. Account of goods taken on credit, bill.

adj. Driven away, caused to disappear; banished; scared, terrified; bewildered; wild-looking; separated; alienated, estranged; displeased, offended; dissatisfied; indifferent; weary (of); disgusted (with); disheartened; sad, sorrowful; — s.f. (S. uććāṭana), Driving away; banishing; breaking (as sleep); separation, estrangement, alieṅation; terror, scared look or appearance; longing to be separated; vexation; sadness, sorrow; weariness; disgust
subs: v.n. To be alienated or estranged; to be scared; to feel aversion, weariness, disgust, &c.
v.n. To be scared, terrified, &c.; to be averse (to); disgusted (with); weary (of), &c.
v.n. To be driven away, banished; to be alienated, &c. ( = ućāṭ khānā, q.v.); to be displeased or offended (with -se), be disgusted (with), &c.

def: v.t. To drive or turn away (from), to scare away; to dissuade (from); to banish, to break or disturb (sleep); to separate, divide; to cause to rebound; to cause to splash; to dishearten, discourage; to sadden, to make anxious — ućṭā-denā = ućāṭnā intensified. (See ućṭānā.)

v.n. To go away (from, -se); to withdraw (from); to separate, part (from); to divide; to be separated, detached, severed; to come off (as plaster from a wall); to be dropped or lost; to be alienated (from); to be displeased or offended (with); to be weary (of); to be banished, broken, or disturbed (as sleep); to be scared away; to rebound (as a sword striking a hard body obliquely), glance off, recoil, spring back; to spring, bound, leap again; to slip away: — ućaṭ-jānā = ućaṭnā intensified.
s.m. Talking in one's sleep; dreaming uneasily; bawling aloud, or raving, in sleep; incubus or nightmare.
adj. High, lofty, exalted; ascendant, predominant; — s.m. The apex of the orbit of a planet; the sign of the zodiac in which a planet obtains its most powerful influence
subs: s.m. High rank, station, or office: ućća-pad-dhārī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Holding high office or position; — a person of rank; dignitary; magnate
( = S. grahas), Ascendant planet; good or propitious star.

def: s.f. Height, elevation.
s.m. = اچارن uććāraṇ, q.v. s.m. Pronunication; utterance; articulation; enunciation; expression, delivery
subs: v.t. To pronounce, &c., to speak, express.

def: s.m. Pronunication; utterance; articulation; enunciation; expression, delivery
subs: v.t. To pronounce, &c., to speak, express.

def: part. Pronounced, uttered, articulated.
= اچارت uććārit, q.v. part. Pronounced, uttered, articulated.
v.t. To pronounce, &c. = uććāraṇ karnā, q.v.s.v. uććāraṇ. s.m. Pronunication; utterance; articulation; enunciation; expression, delivery
subs: v.t. To pronounce, &c., to speak, express.

def: v.n. To rise (as pustules, boils, &c.), to come off, separate (as the skin from the flesh, crust or scales, &c.); — to be pronounced, uttered, spoken, &c.
adv. On high, aloft.
s.f. Height, loftiness; exaltation.
s.m. The act of driving away, alienating, &c. (See ućāṭ.)

ety: S. उत्सव; cf. also S. उत्साह
def: s.m. Festival, jubilee, public demonstration of joy; joy, gladness, merry-making
subs: s.m. One who exults; boaster, braggart.

def: part. Destroyed, ruined, laid waste; deficient.
s.m. Remains of food, leavings, fragments, scraps.
s.m. Suffocation, choking
subs: or ho-jānā, v.n. To be suffocated or choked.

def: s.m. Breath, breathing; hope, expectation; division of a book.
v.t. To separate one thing from another; to peel off (skin, scales, scabs, &c.); to pare.

ety: S. उत्सव; cf. also S. उत्साह
def: s.m. Festival, jubilee, public demonstration of joy; joy, gladness, merry-making
subs: s.m. One who exults; boaster, braggart.

def: s.f. Motion upwards; spring upwards: tossing up or about, &c. (see ućhālnā)
subs: and ućhāl-ćhakkā, s.f. A forward wanton woman who exposes her bosom; a harlot.

def: s.f. Difference; discrepancy; incongruity.

v.n. To leap, bound, spring, jump; to spout up, fly out, gush out, spurt out; to fly or bounce up, shoot up; to dance about, cut capers; to fly (at, -par), spring (upon); to fly into a passion, to dance with rage
subs: v.n. To spring (upon, -par); to break into a passion, fly into a rage
s.f. Leaping and jumping; hopping and skipping; gambols; dancing and cutting capers.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit उच्छलति (ucchalati) (compare उच्चलति (uccalati)). Cognate with Punjabi ਉੱਛਲਣਾ (ucchalṇā) / اُچّھَلݨا (ucchalṇā), Gujarati ઊછળવું (ūchaḷvũ), Marathi उसळणे (usaḷṇe).

def: v.t. To throw up, toss up; to dandle, fling up (a child); to cast up, vomit; to toss about, bandy, treat with disrespect, bring discredit on
subs: v.t. (intens. of ućhālnā), To throw or toss up, &c.

def: s.f. The multiplication table ( = pahāṛā).
s.m. Lap; slope of a mountain; a roof.

ety: S. उत्सव; cf. also S. उत्साह
def: s.m. Festival, jubilee, public demonstration of joy; joy, gladness, merry-making
subs: s.m. One who exults; boaster, braggart.

def: s.m. Covering, curtain, screen.
= اچهلنا ućhalnā, q.v. v.t. To throw up, toss up; to dandle, fling up (a child); to cast up, vomit; to toss about, bandy, treat with disrespect, bring discredit on
subs: v.t. (intens. of ućhālnā), To throw or toss up, &c.

def: = اکهڙنا ukhaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To root up, eradicate, extirpate, pluck up or out, tear up or off, dig up; to break off, disjoin, sunder; to dislocate, displace; to take out, extract, draw out; to detach, separate, remove; to set (one) against, estrange, alienate; entice away, win over (from), buy off; to drive away, put away, do away with, dismiss; to upset, overturn, subvert; to scatter, disperse; to demolish, desolate, destroy, lay waste, devastate, exterminate
s.f. Holding aloof (from), avoiding, shunning; retirement, retreat.
v.n. To withdraw (from, -se), to tire or be weary (of); to be offended (with), &c. ( = ućaṭnā, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be thrown up, &c.; also = ućhālnā, q.v. v.t. To throw up, toss up; to dandle, fling up (a child); to cast up, vomit; to toss about, bandy, treat with disrespect, bring discredit on
subs: v.t. (intens. of ućhālnā), To throw or toss up, &c.

def: adj. Proper, meet, just, right, fit, becoming, seemly, befitting, suitable; convenient; expedient, obligatory, necessary; acceptable; advisable.
s.f. Propriety, fitness, befittingness, obligatoriness, &c. (See ućit.)
s.m. The reel on which the silk with which kites are flown is wound.
s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, q.v. s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
v.t. To lift or raise up; to jerk up; to shrug (the shoulders).
v.t. To cause to be separated (one thing from another), to have (one thing) separated (from another).
s.m. A moth ( = patang).

v.t. = اچاٿنا ućāṭnā, q.v. v.t. To drive or turn away (from), to scare away; to dissuade (from); to banish, to break or disturb (sleep); to separate, divide; to cause to rebound; to cause to splash; to dishearten, discourage; to sadden, to make anxious — ućṭā-denā = ućāṭnā intensified. (See ućṭānā.)

s.m. An otter; a disputed point (this sense originates from a story of otters disputing over a distribution of their prey); — a stupid fellow, dolt, blockhead, fool, simpleton (cf. ūt); — adj. Voracious (as an otter), ready to devour; ready, eager
s.m. Wood, timber; a sprig; a staff, stick, rod; — aloes-wood; — a lute
subs: s.m. A vessel in which aloes-wood is burned (for fumigation).

def: insep. prefix = उत् ut, q.v.
adj. Grey; brown; purple; — s.m. Grey, brown, or purple colour (see audāt).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀸𑀬𑀇 (uddāyaï, “is polished, is very clear”), from an unattested Sanskrit *उद्दात (*uddāta, “cleansed, bright, clear”), from उद्- (ud-, “over, above”) + दात (dāta, “cleansed, purified”), the latter from the root दै (dai, “to purify, cleanse”).

def: s.m. The ocean.
s.m. Example, illustration, instance; simile; comparison; an opposite argument; (in Logic) one of the five modes of reasoning.
s.f. = اداهت udāhat, q.v. s.f. Brownness; brown; purple.
s.f. Brownness; brown; purple.
s.f. Brownness, &c. = udāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Brownness; brown; purple.
s.m. Water; the ceremony of pouring water on obsequies; ablution.
s.m. One of the three or five vital airs (that which rises up the throat and passes into the head).
adj. Noble, gentle; illustrious; generous, liberal; munificent; great; best; — unperplexed
subs: adj. Of liberal dealing; of a generous disposition, noble-minded.

def: s.m. Belly, stomach; bowels; womb; cavity; the interior or inside of anything; 'the inner man'; protuberance; 'corporation'; — slaughter, war
subs: (i.e. udara+āmay), s.m. Disease of the belly; dysentery; diarrhœa
adj. & s.m. Demon-bellied; voracious; gluttonous; — a glutton
s.f. The line of hair on the belly

def: corr. of ادهر udhar, q.v. adv. There, thither, on that side; that way; away
subs: adv. From that, or the other, side; that way
v.n. To turn away the ear; turn a deaf ear.

def: = H ادڙاگدڙا उदड़ा गुदड़ा udṛā-gudṛā, گدڙ or گدڙا redupl.), s.m. rags and tatters; old rags; cast-off clothes.
adj. Abdominal; pregnant; &c. = udarin, q.v.: — s.f. Enlargement of the abdomen from dropsy or from flatulency.
adj. Having a large belly; pot-bellied, fat, corpulent; abdominal. (See udarī.)
adj. Having a large belly; pot-bellied, fat, corpulent; abdominal. (See udarī.)
s.f. A corpulent woman; a pregnant woman.
s.f. Liberality, generosity, munificence.

adj. Indifferent (to, -se), unconcerned, apathetic; unsettled in mind; retired, lone, solitary; forlorn, dejected, sad, sorrowful; dull, dispirited, cast-down; grieved, displeased (with); sullen, cross; not bright, dull, sombre, faded
subs: retiredness, loneliness; asceticism; — sickly crop (in agricult.)
udās paṛnā or paṛ-jānā, and udās honā, v.n. To be sad, sorrowful, dejected, &c.; to be grieved (at, -se), displeased (with); to turn (against), revolt (from); to be dull, dim, or lustreless.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi اداس / उदास (udāsa, “indifferent”) (c. 1503), a learned borrowing from Sanskrit उदास (udāsa).

def: s.m. A cord worn as a shoulder-belt by Mohammadan devotees (āzād).
s.f. Sadness, sorrow; despondency, dejection; retirement; loneliness; solitude; dulness, dimness
subs: v.n. Dejection or gloom to cloud (the face); to be clouded with sorrow; to become dull or sad.

def: adj. Unsettled; retired, solitary, lonely; dejected, forlorn, sad; — s.m. A dejected person, &c.; ascetic; anchorite; hermit; a class of religious mendicants; (in Music) the bass

adj. Sitting apart; indifferent; free from affection: s.m. One who is completely detached from the world, whose family is unknown, and who has neither friend nor foe; hermit; misanthrope; stoical indifferent person; one who is neither a friend nor a foe; an umpire; an order of religious mendicants in the Panjāb.
s.f. Indifference, carelessness, apathy.

s.m. Rising (of the sun, &c.); dawn, sunrise; ascent; appearance, advent; the East (as opposed to asta, the West); the eastern mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise ( = udayāćal); light, splendour; prosperity, success
subs: (i.e. udaya+aćala), s.m. The mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise
( = S. udayāsta), udayast-tak, adv. From sunrise to sunset, the whole day; from the East to the West, over the whole world
To rise (as the sun, &c.), to dawn; to appear, be manifest; to begin, arise, originate; to spring up, sprout.

def: = उदय uday, q.v. s.m. Rising (of the sun, &c.); dawn, sunrise; ascent; appearance, advent; the East (as opposed to asta, the West); the eastern mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise ( = udayāćal); light, splendour; prosperity, success
subs: (i.e. udaya+aćala), s.m. The mountain from behind which the sun is supposed to rise
( = S. udayāsta), udayast-tak, adv. From sunrise to sunset, the whole day; from the East to the West, over the whole world
To rise (as the sun, &c.), to dawn; to appear, be manifest; to begin, arise, originate; to spring up, sprout.

def: adj. Rising, ascending; prosperous, flourishing.

= ادويگ udveg, q.v. s.m. Perturbation, anxiety, trouble, care, solicitude, perplexity, agitation, uneasiness; bewilderment; fear, apprehension, consternation; exertion; haste, hurry, impetuosity; — the fruit of the Areca catechu, betel nut.
s.m. A being born; springing up (as plants from seed); birth, production.
adj. Sprouting, germinating; vegetable.
s.m. See s.v. ūd.
s.m. Recollection; recognition.
s.m. = اديم udyam, q.v. s.m. Effort, exertion, endeavour; labour, toil, pains, trouble; employment, calling, business, trade
subs: To use effort or exertion, to exert oneself, to endeavour, take pains or trouble; to labour, toil; to follow a trade or calling.

def: adj. Rebellious, in revolt
subs: To rebel, revolt, rise in rebellion, &c.

def: s.m. Direction, indication, sign, trace; search, inquiry; scope; ascertainment; report, information, tidings; exemplification, example; quarter, region, place, foreign land or country ( = pardes).
s.m. Anything to which one refers or which one has in view; anything noteworthy; (in Gram.) the subject.
s.m. A disease occasioned by the mounting of wind upwards; act of gasping or panting; asthma; flatulency; indigestion; plethora of the vessels of the head; headache.
s.m. Debt; loan; trust, credit; obligation; deduction; discharge, release, extrication, deliverance, redemption, salvation; raising up from any place or state
subs: v.t. To lend; give on credit, or give credit; to discharge a debt
v.t. To rescue, extricate (from, -se), save, &c.; to effect the removal (of, -kā); to remove, do away with
(-ke ūpar), To treat (one) as a mortal enemy, to become (one's) mortal enemy
v.t. To contract a debt; to borrow, take a loan; to take on credit
v.t. To ask on loan, to borrow
uddhār or udhār honā or ho-jānā
To be in debt; to owe; to be out on loan, be lent; to be released (from, -se, an obligation, &c.); to be liberated, discharged; to be delivered or extricated (from danger, difficulties, &c.).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀸𑀭 (uddhāra), from Sanskrit उद्धार (uddhārá, “act of raising”).

def: adj. & s.m. Delivering, liberating, relieving; — deliverer, liberator, &c.
v.t. To deliver, liberate, discharge, extricate (from, -se).
part. adj. Puffed up, vain, haughty, arrogant, rude, ill-behaved; — s.m. A king's wrestler.
s.m. Blazing or glowing of a luminous body; illumination; exciting, flaming (as passion).
part. Illuminated.

ety: from uḍṅā; ū or o = S. affix उक
def: s.m.f. One who goes flying about from place to place, a gad-about
subs: To become disgraced; to lose one's good name.

def: s.m. Pointing to or at, direction; track; search, quest
s.m. Throwing out, spitting out; vomiting, belching; saliva, eructation; exhalation; sound; roar.
part. Thrown up, vomited; risen up, sprung up, grown.
s.m. Opening, unlocking; opener, instrument for opening (as a key, &c.); the rope and bucket of a well, a leather bucket used for drawing water.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Done with effort; exerted; opened, unlocked.
v.n. To be ruined, destroyed, spoiled; to be squandered, dissipated, &c.; to be carried off, to run away, elope, be seduced (a woman); to be full of lust, long for carnal intercourse (a woman)
subs: and is the more common form.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *उद्ध्वलति (*uddhvalati), from उद्- (ud-) + ह्वल॑ति (hválati, “to go astray”), or from उद्- (ud-) + *ध्वलति (*dhvalati).

def: s.m. Noise, uproar ( = dhūm), disturbance, commotion, ado, tumult, rebellion; quarrel, brawl
subs: ūdham karnā, ūdham maćānā, v.n. To make a great noise, raise a commotion, make an ado, &c.

def: adj. Noisy, riotous, turbulent; quarrelsome; — s.m. A noisy person, &c.
s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, q.v. s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
adv. There, thither, on that side; that way; away
subs: adv. From that, or the other, side; that way
v.n. To turn away the ear; turn a deaf ear.

def: s.m. Debtor.

v.n. To be taken out or off; to be or become unrolled, unravelled, untwisted, unwound; to be torn off, be laid or ripped open, be excoriated, peeled, flayed (the skin); to open out, unravel, come undone (sewing); to be untied, be separated, be divorced; to be ruined or destroyed; to be spent, exhausted, dissipated, squandered
subs: and is the more common form.

def: s.m.f. (?) Unravelling; unweaving; unpicking (stitches), &c.: udheṛ-bun, s.f. Lit. 'Unpicking and weaving'; (hence), perplexity, bewilderment, embarrassment, confusion, dilemma; uneasiness, disquiet, anxiety, solicitude, concern; distress, grief; — devising, planning; deliberation, anxious thought; scheme, plan, device.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀅𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀯𑁂𑀟 (*addhaveḍa), from Sanskrit अर्ध (ardha, “half”) + *वेड (*veḍa) with the second term possibly related to वेला (velā, “time, occasion”). Cognate with Punjabi ਅਧੇੜ (adheṛ), Gujarati આધેડ (ādheḍ).

def: v.n. = udhaṛnā, q.v.; — 2˚ v.t. To undo, unsew, unravel, unpick (sewing), untwist, unknot; to pull to pieces; to lay open, unfold, disclose, expose; to strip off, skin, flay, pluck, rip, excoriate, bark; to pull off the upper part of (as the crust of a cake, or the roof of a house); to pull up; open again (as a grave, &c.); to ruin, destroy; to impoverish, reduce to poverty; to waste, squander, dissipate, scatter, make away with, plunder
subs: intens. of udheṛnā, v.t. To pluck out, pull up or off, rip open, lay quite bare, &c. (see udheṛnā).

def: = ادهيڙنا udheṛnā, q.v.
adj. (f. of udhlā), Lascivious, lustful, lecherous.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Unrolled, unravelled, undone, untied; excoriated, peeled, flayed, &c. (See next.)
= ادهڙا udhṛā, q.v.
s.f. A light shade of blue or grey, or of purple.
adj. Of wood; of aloes-wood.
part. adj. Risen (as the sun, &c.); shining; distinguished, conspicuous (as valour, &c.).

adj. (f. -ā), Elevated, high; acutely accented; great, illustrious; generous, liberal, bountiful; dear, beloved; — s.m. The acute accent; a high or sharp tone; an ornament in rhetoric; a kind of musical instrument; a large drum; a gift or donation.
s.m. Madness, mania; extravagance; frenzy; excitement; lust, lasciviousness; pride, conceit; show, ostentation; vice, depravity
subs: v.n. To be stirred with morbid desire, lust or lasciviousness; to lust (after); hanker (after); to be filled with affectation; to make a display; to swagger.

def: adj. & s.m. Mad, frantic; morbidly desirous, lustful, lascivious, libidinous, lewd, wanton
adj. & s.m. Mad, frantic; morbidly desirous, lustful, lascivious, libidinous, lewd, wanton
